commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Passat Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI Turbo Failure - Why BMR/BKP Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Passat B6/B7 2.0 TDI (2005-2014, BMR, BKP, CFFB codes)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seal wear allows oil into intake/exhaust, causing smoke, power loss, and eventual bearing failure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty wastegate actuator, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if turbo internals undamaged; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, strip, replace bearings/seals, balance, refit
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life 30-50k miles if block undamaged; not for catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when debris entered engine; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: If smoke is present or turbo noise is metallic, debris has likely entered the engine - replacement is safer and often cheaper long-term than repeated turbo repairs on a compromised block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BMR/BKP engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat

problems.h4: EGR Valve Failure - Why Passat Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Passat 1.9/2.0 TDI (2000-2024, AVF, BKP, CFFB codes)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve cokes with carbon, restricting flow and triggering limp mode to protect the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, ultrasonic clean, or fit blanking plate (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may trigger fault codes; not suitable for Euro 6 models

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new OEM or quality aftermarket valve, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function 40-70k miles; address underlying carbon causes

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated EGR components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if carbon damage affected intake manifold or turbo

problems.recommendation: For early symptoms (slight power loss, occasional limp mode), cleaning or replacement is cost-effective. If the engine has been running rich due to EGR failure, check for secondary damage before deciding.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CFFB EGR-related Volkswagen Passat engine replacement

problems.h4: DPF Blockage - Why Short Journeys Kill Passat Diesels
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI Euro 5/6 (2008-2024, CFFB, CFGB, EA288 codes)
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter cannot regenerate on short trips, leading to ash buildup, restricted exhaust, and eventual engine protection mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, high-rev drive cycle or dealer software regen
repairOptions.longevity: Only works if filter not physically blocked; temporary if usage unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, chemically clean or replace filter, reset systems
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function 30-60k miles; illegal for road use if removed entirely

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best when DPF failure caused backpressure damage to turbo or valves

problems.recommendation: If your Passat is used primarily for short journeys, a rebuilt engine with updated DPF strategy may be more reliable long-term than repeated cleaning cycles.
problems.cta: Compare EA288 rebuilt engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat

problems.h4: 3.6 V6 Timing Chain Failure - Catastrophic Risk on BDP Engines
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Passat R36/CC 3.6 V6 (2005-2014, BDP code)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling on startup and potential valve/piston contact if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guides before chain skips
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable with early diagnosis; requires engine front removal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V6 with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once chain has jumped; prevents repeat catastrophic failure

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start from a BDP engine, stop driving immediately - continued operation risks total engine destruction. Replacement is the only safe path post-failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for BDP V6 Volkswagen Passat engine replacement

problems.h4: 1.8 TSI Timing Chain Tensioner - Early CDA Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Passat 1.8 TSI (2008-2012, CDA, CDAA codes)
problems.rootCause: Early tensioner design could fail without warning, allowing chain skip and valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit revised tensioner part, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not viable if chain has already jumped

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.8 TSI with updated timing kit, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if valve timing was lost; eliminates repeat risk

problems.recommendation: For pre-2012 1.8 TSI Passats, budget for a preventative tensioner update. If the engine has rattled or lost power, replacement is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CDA engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Passat
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing turbo failure, EGR issues, or timing chain concerns, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Passat specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Passat Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Passat and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Volkswagen Passat Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for your Volkswagen Passat is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Volkswagen Passat Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Fully stripped, cleaned, inspected and rebuilt with new bearings, seals and gaskets to restore like-new performance.
types.frontDescription: Fully stripped, cleaned, inspected and rebuilt with new bearings, seals and gaskets to restore like-new performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, mileage and supplier location.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Passat engines undergo full disassembly, machining where needed, and replacement of wear items. Ideal for BMR, BKP, CFFB diesels and 1.8T petrols.
backBullets: Includes new bearings, seals, gaskets, oil pump
Best value for engines with good block condition
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Volkswagen Passat engine prices

types.title: Volkswagen Passat Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points like timing chains or turbo seals.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points like timing chains or turbo seals.
types.frontDisclaimer: Upgrades may affect final price; confirm specs with supplier.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Passat engines feature enhanced components for common issues - upgraded tensioners for BDP V6, revised turbo seals for 2.0 TDI.
backBullets: Suitable for high-mileage or previously repaired units
types.cta: Get rebuilt Volkswagen Passat engine quotes

types.title: Volkswagen Passat Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage donor engines from UK breakers, tested and supplied with basic warranty for budget-conscious replacements.

types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and condition vary; inspect report before purchase.
types.backDescription: Used Passat engines offer the most affordable route for AVF, AWX, early BKP units where block condition is verified and mileage documented.
backBullets: Source from accident-damaged but mechanically sound donors
Ideal for older B5/B5.5 Passats or tight budgets
3-6 month warranty typical; confirm terms per supplier
types.cta: Check used Volkswagen Passat engine availability

types.title: Volkswagen Passat Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec remanufacturing to original tolerances with all components renewed or replaced to meet OEM standards.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec remanufacturing to original tolerances with all components renewed or replaced to meet OEM standards.
types.frontDisclaimer: Premium option; lead times may apply for specialist units.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured Passat engines are built to factory specifications with all components renewed - ideal for late-model B8 EA288 or performance V6 units.
backBullets: Every component inspected, machined or replaced as needed
Matches OEM performance and emissions compliance
Best for newer Passats or where warranty coverage is critical

types.title: Volkswagen Passat Refurbished Engines
types.description: Targeted refurbishment addressing specific known faults while retaining serviceable original components where possible.
types.frontDescription: Targeted refurbishment addressing specific known faults while retaining serviceable original components where possible.
types.frontDisclaimer: Scope varies by supplier; confirm exactly what is replaced.
types.backDescription: Refurbished Passat engines focus on fixing the documented failure - e.g., new turbo and seals for BMR, updated tensioner for CDA - without full rebuild cost.
backBullets: Cost-effective for engines with single-point failures
Requires accurate diagnosis to ensure correct scope

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Passat Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI Bluemotion engine was introduced for B7 Passats to meet Euro 5 emissions while improving fuel economy - a popular choice for fleet and high-mileage drivers, though turbo reliability requires attention.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo failure common at 95,000 miles; EGR valve coking; DPF blockage on short journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Passat 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary 1.9 PD TDI powered early B5.5 Passats and remains one of the most reliable diesel engines ever fitted - simple, robust, and still widely sought for replacements today.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve coking; injector seal wear; dual-mass flywheel rattles at high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Passat 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI is the backbone of the Passat range across B6, B7 and B8 generations - powerful and efficient but requiring proactive maintenance of turbo, EGR and timing components.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo oil seal failure 80-120k miles; EGR cooler leaks; timing chain tensioner wear (BMR); piezo injector failure (CFFB)
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Passat 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The downsized 1.4 TSI with turbo (and supercharger on early units) delivered strong economy but introduced complexity - ideal for urban Passats where maintenance history is documented.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure fuel pump failure; timing chain tensioner on early units; carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Passat 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The iconic 1.8T turbo powered early B5 Passats and later evolved into the 1.8 TSI - responsive and tunable but requiring strict oil change intervals to avoid sludge and timing issues.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt/chain tensioner failure; turbo oil feed coking; diverter valve wear causing boost loss
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Passat 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TSI delivers strong performance for R-Line and CC variants - refined and powerful but requiring attention to timing chain and high-pressure fuel system maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear on early EA888; high-pressure fuel pump; carbon buildup on direct injection models
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Passat 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Passat 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 3.6 VR6 delivered 280-300hp to R36 and CC models - smooth, powerful and distinctive, but timing chain wear demands proactive replacement to avoid catastrophic failure. Not sure which engine size your Volkswagen Passat needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Volkswagen Passat specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain wear audible from 100,000km; chain tensioner failure causing valve damage; oil consumption on high-mileage units

families: BMR - 2.0 TDI 170hp (B6), most searched code, timing chain/turbo risks
BKP - 2.0 TDI 140hp (B6), widespread EGR and injector seal issues
CFFB - 2.0 TDI 140/150hp (B7), piezo injector failure common
AVF/AWX - 1.9 TDI 130hp (B5.5), legendary reliability, high used demand
CAYC - 1.6 TDI 105hp (B7), turbo failure at ~95k miles documented
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and high-mileage capability
Turbo failure £850-1,700 repair cost on 2.0 TDI units
EGR valve coking causing limp mode and power loss
DPF blockage on short-journey urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Passat Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volkswagen Passat Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Passats span 1.4-3.6L across generations, with 1.8T/TSI and 2.0 TSI units most common in replacement searches due to timing and fuel system concerns.
items.descriptor: Petrol Passats span 1.4-3.6L across generations, with 1.8T/TSI and 2.0 TSI units most common in replacement searches due to timing and fuel system concerns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Passat Petrol Engine Families
families: AEB/ATW - 1.8T 150hp (B5), iconic turbo but sludge/timing risks
CAXA - 1.4 TSI 122hp (B7), HPFP and timing chain tensioner issues
CDA/CDAA - 1.8 TSI 160/178hp (B6/B7), early tensioner failure risk
EA888 - 2.0 TSI 217-272hp (B7/B8), refined but carbon buildup on DI
BDP - 3.6 VR6 280hp (B6/B7), timing chain wear from 100k km
foundIn: B8 (2014-2024) - EA888 2.0 TSI, 1.4 eTSI mild hybrid
knownFor: Responsive turbo performance and smoother refinement than diesel
Timing chain tensioner failures on early 1.8/2.0 TSI units
Carbon buildup on direct injection intake valves requiring cleaning
High-pressure fuel pump failures on 1.4/2.0 TSI engines
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Passat Petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Passat Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The latest B9 Passat (2024+) is petrol-only with eTSI mild hybrid and eHybrid plug-in options - earlier B8 offered GTE PHEV variants with specialist replacement considerations.
items.descriptor: The latest B9 Passat (2024+) is petrol-only with eTSI mild hybrid and eHybrid plug-in options - earlier B8 offered GTE PHEV variants with specialist replacement considerations.
families: 48-volt starter-generator recovers braking energy for eTSI models
Electric motor provides torque assistance and short EV-only range on PHEV
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and specialist coding
importantNotes: No pure diesel option on B9 generation sold in UK
PHEV battery health check recommended during engine replacement
Specialist supplier availability more limited than standard engines

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Passat Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Passat's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.preview: B5 Launch: 1.8T Turbo & 1.9 TDI PD Debut
years.description: The original B5 Passat launched with the iconic 1.8T petrol and robust 1.9 PD TDI diesel - engines that would define the model's reputation for decades.
keyChanges: B5 platform introduced with longitudinal engine layout
1.8T AEB turbo petrol and 1.9 TDI AFN PD diesel headline engines
Estate (Variant) body style added for commercial/fleet appeal
knownFor: 1.9 TDI legendary reliability and low running costs
1.8T tunability but requires strict oil change discipline
Timing belt critical service interval at 60k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2000 Volkswagen Passat replacement engines

years.description: The B5.5 refresh brought updated styling and the widely adopted AVF/AWX 1.9 TDI engines - now the most sought-after used Passat engines in the UK.
keyChanges: Facelifted front/rear styling and interior upgrades
AVF/AWX 1.9 TDI (130hp) become fleet and private favourite
1.8T AWM (190hp) introduced for sportier variants
knownFor: AVF/AWX engines renowned for 200k+ mile longevity with care
EGR valve coking common but easily addressed
Timing belt replacement critical at 60k miles or 5 years
years.cta: Compare rebuilt AVF/AWX engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat
years.ticker: Passat 1.9 TDI engine replacement • AVF engine for sale UK • Passat timing belt cost

years.description: The all-new B6 Passat introduced the 2.0 TDI BKP (140hp) and BMR (170hp) engines - powerful but bringing new failure patterns that dominate today's replacement searches.
keyChanges: New PQ46 platform with transverse engine layout
2.0 TDI common rail preparation (PD still used initially)
DSG gearbox options increase complexity of drivetrain failures
knownFor: BMR timing chain tensioner wear 90-110k miles
Turbo oil seal failure on BKP/BMR £850-1,700 repair
DSG mechatronic issues often quoted alongside engine work
years.cta: Get quotes for B6 Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI engine replacement
years.ticker: BMR engine replacement cost • Passat turbo failure symptoms • BKP EGR valve limp mode

years.preview: B6 LCI: Common Rail TDI & 1.8 TSI Introduction
years.description: The B6 facelift brought cleaner common rail TDI engines and the complex 1.8 TSI petrol - improving emissions but introducing new failure points for owners to manage.
keyChanges: Common rail injection replaces PD on 2.0 TDI (CFFB/CFGB)
1.8 TSI CDA/CDAA petrol engines introduced with chain drive
Euro 5 compliance begins for new registrations
knownFor: Piezo injector failure on CFFB expensive to replace
Early 1.8 TSI timing chain tensioner failure risk
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey diesel use
years.cta: Compare rebuilt CFFB engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat
years.ticker: CFFB injector failure • Passat 1.8 TSI timing chain • DPF blocked Passat repair cost

years.preview: B7 Generation: EA288 Preparation & 1.6 TDI Expansion
years.description: The B7 Passat refined the common rail TDI range with CFFB/CFGB and added the efficient but turbo-vulnerable 1.6 TDI CAYC for fleet buyers.
keyChanges: B7 platform update with revised styling and tech
1.6 TDI CAYC (105hp) added for low-emission fleets
3.6 V6 BDP offered in R36 and CC performance variants
knownFor: CAYC turbo failure common at ~95,000 miles
BDP V6 timing chain wear from 100,000km catastrophic risk
EGR cooler leaks on CFFB causing coolant loss
years.cta: Get quotes for B7 Volkswagen Passat engine replacement
years.ticker: CAYC turbo failure 95k miles • BDP timing chain replacement cost • Passat R36 engine rebuild

years.preview: B8 Generation: EA288 Modular Diesel & Petrol Return
years.description: The MQB-based B8 Passat introduced the modular EA288 2.0 TDI (04L131501C) and revived petrol options - cleaner but more complex, with AdBlue and emissions system considerations.
keyChanges: MQB platform with transverse EA288 modular diesel engines
Petrol engines return with 1.4 eTSI mild hybrid and 2.0 TSI options
GTE plug-in hybrid variant added for company car appeal
mainEngines: EA211 evo - 1.5L eTSI mild hybrid (150hp, late B8)
knownFor: AdBlue injector/NOx sensor faults on EA288
EGR cooler internal leaks causing coolant/oil mix
Timing chain improved but still monitor on high-mileage EA888
years.cta: Compare rebuilt EA288 engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat
years.ticker: EA288 AdBlue fault • Passat B8 engine replacement cost • GTE hybrid battery check

years.preview: Present - B9 Generation: Petrol-Only & Electrified Future
years.description: The all-new B9 Passat is petrol-only in the UK, featuring eTSI mild hybrid and eHybrid plug-in powertrains - marking the end of diesel for new Passat sales.
keyChanges: Diesel discontinued for UK market; petrol-electric focus
eTSI 48V mild hybrid standard on most trims
eHybrid PHEV offers ~30 miles electric-only range
knownFor: New generation; long-term reliability data still emerging
Hybrid system complexity requires specialist diagnostics
Battery health critical for PHEV residual values
years.cta: Enquire about B9 Volkswagen Passat eTSI/eHybrid availability

faq.h2: Volkswagen Passat Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Volkswagen Passat engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volkswagen Passat engines typically cost £950-£2,400 supply-only depending on engine code, with 2.0 TDI BMR units averaging £1,110-£1,755. Supply & fit adds £450-£950 labour.
keyPoints: Used engines: £500-£1,500 supply-only
Reconditioned: £1,100-£2,400 with 12-month warranty
2.0 TDI BMR most common replacement at £1,110-£1,755
Labour for supply & fit typically £450-£950 extra
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volkswagen Passat engine prices for your variant

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for a Volkswagen Passat?
items.answer: The 1.9 TDI AVF/AWX (2000-2005) is widely regarded as the most reliable Volkswagen Passat engine, with many exceeding 200,000 miles with proper maintenance.
keyPoints: 1.9 TDI AVF/AWX legendary for longevity and simplicity
2.0 TDI BKP/BMR reliable if turbo/EGR maintained proactively
Avoid early 1.8 TSI CDA (2008-2010) due to tensioner risk
Regular oil changes critical for all turbo Passat engines
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable 1.9 TDI Volkswagen Passat engine replacement

items.question: Why is my Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI in limp mode?
items.answer: Limp mode on Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI engines is most commonly caused by EGR valve coking, turbo actuator faults, or DPF blockage restricting exhaust flow.
keyPoints: EGR valve carbon buildup triggers P0401/P0402 codes
Turbo wastegate actuator failure causes boost loss
DPF blockage from short journeys triggers protection mode
Diagnostic scan essential before deciding repair vs replace
items.cta: Compare EGR/turbo repair costs for your Volkswagen Passat

items.question: Can I fit a Golf engine into my Volkswagen Passat?
items.answer: Yes, EA288 2.0 TDI engines from Golf Mk7/7.5 can fit B7/B8 Volkswagen Passat models with matching ECU coding, but compatibility depends on exact engine code and chassis.
keyPoints: EA288 2.0 TDI (04L131501C) shares architecture across VW Group
B6/B7 Passat may require ECU adaptation for donor engine
Always verify engine code compatibility via registration lookup
Specialist fitting recommended for cross-model swaps
items.cta: Check engine compatibility for your Volkswagen Passat registration

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Volkswagen Passat?
items.answer: Replacing the engine in a Volkswagen Passat is often worthwhile if the chassis, interior and gearbox are sound - rebuilt units cost £950-£2,400 versus £3,500-£6,000 for new OEM.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine £950-£2,400 vs new OEM £3,500-£6,000
B5/B5.5 Passats with good bodies justify £750-£1,400 1.9 TDI rebuild
Factor in DSG gearbox condition if automatic
Compare quote against vehicle's post-repair market value
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Volkswagen Passat engine replacement

items.question: What causes turbo failure on Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI engines?
items.answer: Turbo failure on Volkswagen Passat 2.0 TDI (BMR/BKP/CFFB) is typically caused by oil seal wear allowing oil into intake/exhaust, often accelerated by infrequent servicing or short journeys.
keyPoints: Oil seal wear allows oil into turbo housing 80-120k miles
Infrequent oil changes accelerate bearing wear
Short journeys prevent proper turbo cool-down cycles
Debris from failed turbo can damage engine internals
items.cta: Compare rebuilt BMR/BKP engine prices for your Volkswagen Passat

items.question: How long does a Volkswagen Passat engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Volkswagen Passat engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; supply & fit installations usually complete within 1-2 days at specialist workshops nationwide.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines: 1-3 day dispatch from UK network
Supply & fit: 1-2 days workshop time typically
Next-day delivery available for urgent breakdowns
Allow extra time for ECU coding on newer EA288 units
items.cta: Find urgent Volkswagen Passat engine supply & fit deals

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Volkswagen Passat engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volkswagen Passat engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at the fitting specialist.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour at fitting specialist
Confirm exact terms with chosen supplier before order
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Volkswagen Passat engine suppliers

items.question: How do I find my Volkswagen Passat engine code?
items.answer: Your Volkswagen Passat engine code is stamped on the engine block near the timing cover, and also listed on your V5C registration document and service book.
keyPoints: Stamped on engine block near timing belt/chain cover
Listed in V5C registration document section D.2
Service book and dealer records contain engine code
Enter registration on EngineMarket for instant identification
items.cta: Identify your Volkswagen Passat engine code instantly

items.question: Are Volkswagen Passat diesel engines ULEZ compliant?
items.answer: Volkswagen Passat diesel engines registered from September 2015 (Euro 6) are ULEZ compliant; pre-2015 Euro 4/5 engines (BKP/BMR/CFFB) may incur daily charges in London and other CAZs.
keyPoints: Euro 6 (Sept 2015+) Passat diesels ULEZ compliant
Euro 4/5 engines (pre-2015) incur £12.50/day London charge
CFFB/CFGB/EA288 codes typically Euro 5/6 compliant
Replacing with Euro 6 engine can restore ULEZ compliance
items.cta: Compare Euro 6 Volkswagen Passat engine replacement options
